name: Build a closed-loop oval track using orthogonal split
description: Tests that the skill applies the orthogonal split algorithm to handle closed-loop geometry that RoadRunner cannot render as a single self-closing lane.
prompt: Create an oval test track (closed loop, roughly 400m circumference, 2 lanes wide) and write it to oval_track.rrhd. The source geometry forms a closed loop. Show the full generated MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB and produces a .rrhd file
Uses perpendicular plane projection to find split points on boundaries (orthogonal split algorithm)
Does NOT add predecessor/successor topology for track maps
Does NOT add ParametricAttributes (marking references) on boundaries for track maps
Splits the closed loop into N>=2 open segments producing more than 2 Lane objects
assertions.expected: The code splits the closed-loop geometry into at least 2 separate open lane segments PER LANE, resulting in more than 2 total Lane objects (e.g., 4 lanes for 2-lane road with N=2 split). Evidence: Lane array with numel >= 4, or loop creating lanes indexed by segment number. NEGATIVE: Only 2 Lane objects (one per lane, unsplit) means no split was performed — this is WRONG. A single self-closing lane geometry (start point equals or nearly equals end point) is also WRONG.

assertions.description: Forces center line endpoints to boundary midpoints at split joints (100% orthogonal enforcement)
assertions.expected: Center line start/end points at each joint are computed as the midpoint of left and right boundary endpoints: cg(1,:) = (leftBnd(1,:) + rightBnd(1,:))/2 or equivalent averaging. Also enforces second/penultimate points via desiredTan and origDist for orthogonality. NEGATIVE: If center line endpoints are NOT derived from boundary midpoints at joints, or if no explicit endpoint enforcement is performed, this is WRONG.

name: Verify orthogonal joints on a closed-loop track
description: Tests that the skill enforces orthogonality at split joints and uses 100% enforcement (not the 50% blend from synthesizeCenterLine).
prompt: Create a D-shaped test track (one straight side, one curved side, closed loop, 300m circumference, single lane). Split it into segments for RRHD and verify the joints are orthogonal. Show the full MATLAB code including any verification/assertion of joint orthogonality.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Verifies orthogonality at joints using dot product check with assertion or tolerance
Uses 100% orthogonal endpoint enforcement via desiredTan on second/penultimate points
Detects closed-loop geometry before splitting and produces multiple Lane segments
assertions.expected: The code first detects that geometry is a closed loop (endpoint proximity check: norm of first-to-last < threshold), THEN splits into N>=2 segments. Evidence: a closed-loop detection condition followed by split logic producing multiple Lane objects. NEGATIVE: If no closed-loop detection is performed, or if a single Lane is produced from the closed geometry, this is WRONG.

assertions.description: Forces center line endpoints to boundary midpoints at joints
assertions.expected: Center line start/end points are computed as the midpoint of left and right boundary endpoints at each joint. Evidence: code like cg(1,:) = (leftGeom(1,:) + rightGeom(1,:))/2 or equivalent averaging. NEGATIVE: If center line endpoints are independently computed without referencing boundary positions at the joint, this is WRONG.

name: Build a racetrack without topology, Z bumps, or marking attributes
description: Tests that the skill knows track-specific guidance: skip topology, skip Z bumps, skip marking attributes. Base model tends to add these by default for multi-lane roads.
prompt: Create a 2-lane racetrack (oval, closed-loop, 500m circumference, 7.4m total width). The source coordinates use local_x/local_y (not geographic). Write to racetrack.rrhd. Show the full MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Does NOT wire predecessor/successor topology for closed-loop tracks
Does NOT apply marking attributes (ParametricAttributes/MarkingReference) on track boundaries
Splits the closed loop into multiple segments rather than building self-closing lanes
assertions.expected: The code detects the closed-loop geometry and splits into N>=2 segments using perpendicular plane projection or equivalent split algorithm. Evidence: N_SPLITS or nSplits variable >= 2, split point computation, multiple Lane objects per lane. NEGATIVE: Building a single lane per driving lane with geometry that starts and ends at the same point (self-closing) is WRONG — RoadRunner cannot render this. Also WRONG: no split at all with 2 Lane objects total for 2 lanes.

assertions.description: Uses N+1 shared boundary pattern per segment: 3 boundary segments for 2 lane segments
assertions.expected: For each split segment, exactly 3 boundaries exist (left edge, shared center, right edge) for the 2 lanes — the N+1 shared boundary pattern. Total boundary objects should be 3*N_SPLITS. The shared/center boundary is referenced as right boundary of lane 1 and left boundary of lane 2. NEGATIVE: If each lane has its own independent left+right boundaries (4 per segment, not shared), this violates the N+1 pattern.

name: Detect closed-loop geometry and decide to split
description: Tests that the skill recognizes when geometry is a closed loop (start approx equals end) and invokes the split algorithm rather than building a single self-closing lane.
prompt: I have boundary geometry where the first and last points are within 0.5m of each other (a closed loop). Build this as an RRHD lane. The left boundary is a circle of radius 50m (100 points) and the right boundary is a circle of radius 53.7m (100 points), both centered at origin. Write to circle_track.rrhd. Show the full MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Detects that geometry is closed-loop using endpoint proximity check
Applies N-way split (N>=2) with perpendicular projection or segment indexing
Does NOT wire predecessor/successor topology for track maps
Produces multiple open lane segments from the closed-loop input without marking attributes
assertions.expected: The output has at least 2 Lane objects where each is an open segment (start != end). No ParametricAttributes or MarkingReference assigned to boundaries. Evidence: Lane array with numel >= 2, loop creating lanes indexed by segment number, no marking code. NEGATIVE: Only 1 Lane object created from the closed-loop input is WRONG — means no split was applied. Adding ParametricAttributes or MarkingReference is also WRONG for track maps.
